---
pagetitle: KDE Analysis | Capstone
title: KDE Analysis using spatstat package
---

![](../../Images/3.png){width="500"}

# Getting Started

The code chunk below uses `p_load()` function of pacman package to check if the required packages have been installed on the computer. If they are, the packages will be launched.

The packages used are:

-   sf package is used for importing, managing, and processing geospatial data.
-   tidyverse package for aspatial data wrangling.
-   spatstat package to perform 1st-order spatial point patterns analysis and derive kernel density estimation (KDE) layer.
-   tmap package is used to visualize geospatial data on maps.

```{r}
pacman::p_load(sf, tidyverse, spatstat, lubridate, gridExtra, fields)
```

In the code chunk below, the [cleaned data files](DataPrep.qmd) are loaded into R.

```{r}
# Load base map
mpsz = st_read(dsn = "../data/geospatial", 
               layer = "MPSZ-2019") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3414)

# Load rds file
pickup_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/pickup_sf.rds"))
dropoff_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/dropoff_sf.rds"))
```

# Convert Pickup and Dropoff location sf dataframes into spatstat’s ppp object format

Convert the sf dataframe to a ppp object using `as.ppp()` function from the spatstat package. The result is a marked planar point pattern. To change a marked planar point pattern to just a planar point pattern, simply remove the marks associated with each point using `marks(pickup_ppp) <- NULL`.

```{r}
pickup_ppp <- as.ppp(pickup_sf)
marks(pickup_ppp) <- NULL

dropoff_ppp <- as.ppp(dropoff_sf)
marks(dropoff_ppp) <- NULL
```

# Create observational window (owin)

When analysing spatial point patterns, it is a good practice to confine the analysis with a geographical area like Singapore boundary. In spatstat, an object called owin is specially designed to represent this polygonal region. The code chunk below is used to create a border of Singapore's land area and convert it into an owin object of spatstat package using `as.owin()`.

```{r}
# Create border of Singapore's land area
mpsz_border <- st_cast(mpsz %>%
                         filter(!PLN_AREA_N %in% c("WESTERN ISLANDS", "NORTH-EASTERN ISLANDS")
                                & REGION_N == "EAST REGION") %>%
                         summarize(), "POLYGON")

# Convert the resulting sf object to an owin object
mpsz_owin <- as.owin(mpsz_border)

plot(mpsz_owin)
```

# Pick-up Locations: Combine point events object and owin object

In the code chunks below, pick-up and drop-off events located within the observational window are extracted.

```{r}
pickup_owin = pickup_ppp[mpsz_owin]
pickup_owin <- rescale(pickup_owin, 1000, "km")

plot(pickup_owin)
```

# Pick-up Locations - Bandwidth Selection

```{r}
#| eval: FALSE
bw_diggle <- density(pickup_owin, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE)

bw_ppl <- density(pickup_owin, sigma=bw.ppl, edge=TRUE)

bw_abram <- densityAdaptiveKernel(pickup_owin, edge=TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| eval: FALSE
# Convert density objects to data frames for ggplot
density_diggle_df <- as.data.frame(bw_diggle)
density_ppl_df <- as.data.frame(bw_ppl)
density_abram_df <- as.data.frame(bw_abram)

# Calculate the range of density values across all estimates
min_density <- min(density_diggle_df$value, density_ppl_df$value, density_abram_df$value)
max_density <- max(density_diggle_df$value, density_ppl_df$value, density_abram_df$value)
# Define a common fill scale with consistent limits
common_fill_scale <- scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Density: Events per square kilometre", 
                                          limits = c(min_density, max_density),
                                          option = "plasma")

# Create ggplot objects
plot_diggle <- ggplot(density_diggle_df, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Fixed Bandwidth Selection using bw.diggle")

plot_ppl <- ggplot(density_ppl_df, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Fixed Bandwidth Selection using bw.ppl")

plot_abram <- ggplot(density_abram_df, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Adaptive Bandwidth using bw.abram")

# Combine plots using patchwork
combined_plot <- (plot_diggle + plot_ppl + plot_abram) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"))

ggsave('pickup_bw_north.png', combined_plot, width = 12, height = 4.5)
```

# Pick-up Locations - Edge correction
```{r}
#| eval: FALSE
kde1 <- densityAdaptiveKernel(pickup_owin, edge=TRUE, diggle=FALSE)
kde2 <- densityAdaptiveKernel(pickup_owin, edge=TRUE, diggle=TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| eval: FALSE
# Convert density objects to data frames for ggplot
density_default <- as.data.frame(kde1)
density_improved <- as.data.frame(kde2)

# Calculate the range of density values across all estimates
min_density <- min(density_default$value, density_improved$value)
max_density <- max(density_default$value, density_improved$value)
# Define a common fill scale with consistent limits
common_fill_scale <- scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Density: Events per square kilometre", 
                                          limits = c(min_density, max_density),
                                          option = "plasma")

# Create ggplot objects
plot_default <- ggplot(density_default, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(), 
        legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm")) +
  ggtitle("Default Edge Correction")

plot_improved <- ggplot(density_improved, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm")) +
  ggtitle("Improved Edge Correction")

# Combine plots using patchwork
combined_plot <- (plot_default + plot_improved) 
combined_plot <- combined_plot + plot_annotation(title = 'Pick-up Events - East Region') + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"))

ggsave('pickup_edge_east.png', combined_plot, width = 12, height = 5.5)
```

# Drop-off Locations: Combine point events object and owin object

```{r}
dropoff_owin = dropoff_ppp[mpsz_owin]
dropoff_owin <- rescale(dropoff_owin, 1000, "km")

plot(dropoff_owin)
```

# Drop-off Locations - Bandwidth Selection

```{r}
#| eval: FALSE
bw_diggle <- density(dropoff_owin, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE)
bw_ppl <- density(dropoff_owin, sigma=bw.ppl, edge=TRUE)
bw_abram <- densityAdaptiveKernel(dropoff_owin, edge=TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| eval: FALSE
# Convert density objects to data frames for ggplot
density_diggle_df <- as.data.frame(bw_diggle)
density_ppl_df <- as.data.frame(bw_ppl)
density_abram_df <- as.data.frame(bw_abram)

# Calculate the range of density values across all estimates
min_density <- min(density_diggle_df$value, density_ppl_df$value, density_abram_df$value)
max_density <- max(density_diggle_df$value, density_ppl_df$value, density_abram_df$value)
# Define a common fill scale with consistent limits
common_fill_scale <- scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Density: Events per square kilometre", 
                                          limits = c(min_density, max_density),
                                          option = "plasma")

# Create ggplot objects
plot_diggle <- ggplot(density_diggle_df, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Fixed Bandwidth Selection using bw.diggle")

plot_ppl <- ggplot(density_ppl_df, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Fixed Bandwidth Selection using bw.ppl")

plot_abram <- ggplot(density_abram_df, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Adaptive Bandwidth using bw.abram")

# Combine plots using patchwork
combined_plot <- (plot_diggle | plot_ppl) / (plot_abram | plot_spacer()) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"))

ggsave('dropoff_bw_central.png', combined_plot, width = 9, height = 10)
```

# Drop-off Locations - Edge correction
```{r}
#| eval: FALSE
kde1 <- densityAdaptiveKernel(dropoff_owin, edge=TRUE, diggle=FALSE)
kde2 <- densityAdaptiveKernel(dropoff_owin, edge=TRUE, diggle=TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| eval: FALSE
# Convert density objects to data frames for ggplot
density_default <- as.data.frame(kde1)
density_improved <- as.data.frame(kde2)

# Calculate the range of density values across all estimates
min_density <- min(density_default$value, density_improved$value)
max_density <- max(density_default$value, density_improved$value)
# Define a common fill scale with consistent limits
common_fill_scale <- scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Density: Events per square kilometre", 
                                          limits = c(min_density, max_density),
                                          option = "plasma")

# Create ggplot objects
plot_default <- ggplot(density_default, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(), 
        legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm")) +
  ggtitle("Default Edge Correction")

plot_improved <- ggplot(density_improved, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  common_fill_scale +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm")) +
  ggtitle("Improved Edge Correction")

# Combine plots using patchwork
combined_plot <- (plot_default + plot_improved) 
combined_plot <- combined_plot  + plot_annotation(title = 'Drop-off Events - East Region') + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"))

ggsave('dropoff_edge_east.png', combined_plot, width = 12, height = 5.5)
```
